title: EHR Scheduling Integration
description: This facility is booking rides reactively — at time of appointment confirmation rather than during initial scheduling. Short lead times significantly reduce driver match reliability and increase cancellation risk.
action_steps: Schedule a workflow review call with their Care Coordination Director
Demo the EHR-integrated booking flow (Epic / Cerner connectors)
Propose a 30-day pilot with lead time SLA tracking
Escalate to Technical Partnerships if EHR integration is not yet live

title: Staff Activation Training
description: Multiple coordinators are provisioned but inactive. Platform was deployed but not adopted — a common hidden failure mode that inflates no-show risk and reduces booking quality.
action_steps: Identify inactive coordinator accounts by name with facility admin
Schedule a 60-minute platform refresher training session
Set 30-day activation goal with facility manager
Follow up with ride volume data to confirm adoption
escalation_path: Flag for Customer Success onboarding team

title: Outcome Reporting Workflow Review
description: This facility has stopped submitting ride outcome reports. Without outcome data, Uber Health cannot improve driver matching models or demonstrate ROI to payers.
action_steps: Review onboarding documentation — confirm they know how to submit reports
Check if coordinator turnover may have broken the workflow
Re-demo the outcome reporting flow in the platform
Set 2-week target: 100% outcome reporting on all completed rides
escalation_path: Flag for Product team (outcome reporting UX)

title: Platform Dispute & Driver Matching Investigation
description: An unusually high share of no-shows are being attributed to driver/platform failures. This may indicate data quality issues, dispute habits, or a genuine matching problem in this market.
action_steps: Pull raw no-show reason codes for this facility (last 60 days)
Interview coordinator about dispute submission patterns
Check driver supply metrics in this facility's geographic area
Escalate to Operations if driver-attributed rate exceeds 40%

title: No-Show Reason Code Audit
description: High ride volume but poor no-show attribution balance suggests coordinators may be selecting incorrect reason codes — or driver-attributed codes are masking scheduling problems.
action_steps: Export last 30 days of no-show records with reason codes
Review with coordinator — are codes being selected correctly?
Provide reason code guide if needed
Monitor NSAB score over next 30-day cycle

title: Full Account Health Review
description: All performance metrics are critically low on a mature account. This pattern indicates a systematic integration failure that requires cross-functional intervention beyond standard account management.
action_steps: Initiate Customer Success escalation within 48 hours
Schedule executive-level review with facility leadership
Audit all provisioned accounts and ride history
Consider temporary pause of expansion discussions until baseline is restored
escalation_path: Escalate to Customer Success — priority review

title: Proactive Outreach: Acute Score Decline
description: This facility's PRS has dropped significantly in a single period. Acute drops often signal a specific triggering event — staff turnover, system change, or a bad patient incident — that can be addressed before it becomes chronic.
action_steps: Contact facility within 48 hours
Ask directly: did anything change in the past 30 days? (staffing, EHR, procedures)
Review sub-metric breakdown to identify the primary driver of decline
Set a 30-day recovery plan with specific targets
escalation_path: Flag for Account Manager urgent review
